paragraphs.en: By Bryan Washington July 12, 2026
paragraphs.zh: 作者：Bryan Washington，2026年7月12日

paragraphs.en: My passengers are old, but rarely senile. Still very much themselves. The Black dude I wheel toward his flight to Paris every other Thursday morning wears a patchwork cardigan and a teal silk scarf, regardless of the season, because, he tells me, the airport’s temperature is always the same.
paragraphs.zh: 我的乘客年纪都不小，但几乎没有人老糊涂。反而依旧很有自己。那个每隔一个周四早上坐飞机去巴黎的黑人大叔，由我推着前往登机口，穿着一件拼色开衫和一条青绿色的丝巾，不分季节。他跟我说，机场里的温度总是一样的。

paragraphs.en: Bet you keep this bitch freezing on purpose, Carl says.
paragraphs.zh: 你肯定是故意把这里弄得这么冷的，Carl这么说。

paragraphs.en: It’s out of my hands, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，这事不在我控制范围内。

paragraphs.en: Whatever, Carl says. With this wardrobe? I’m giving you predictability. You don’t know shit about that.
paragraphs.zh: 行吧，卡尔这么说。靠这间衣柜？我给你的其实是可预见性。你对这点一无所知。

paragraphs.en: Believe me. You’ll appreciate certainty when you’re old, with grandbabies.
paragraphs.zh: 相信我。等你老了，带着孙儿孙女的时候，你会更珍惜确定性。

paragraphs.en: What the fuck am I talking about? he adds. You’ll never have kids.
paragraphs.zh: 我到底在说什么鬼？他补充道。你永远不会有孩子。

paragraphs.en: Read an interview with the author for the story behind the story.
paragraphs.zh: 想了解这篇故事背后的故事，请阅读作者的采访。

paragraphs.en: I just keep pushing Carl along. He’s some kind of pianist. I’ve never heard him play. He insists that escorting him is the only part of my job that matters. When a crowd disembarking a flight from London crosses in front of us, I brake just a little too hard, and Carl’s coffee spills.
paragraphs.zh: 我只是一直在催卡尔快点。他算是个钢琴家吧，我从来没听过他弹过。他坚持说，护送他是我工作中唯一重要的一部分。当一群从伦敦航班下机的乘客走到我们面前时，我把刹车踩得有点狠，于是卡尔的咖啡洒了出来。

paragraphs.en: He turns to me, furious. But I smile.
paragraphs.zh: 他转向我，怒火中烧。但我仍然在笑。

paragraphs.en: We’re silent the rest of our way to the gate.
paragraphs.zh: 我们在去登机口的其余路上一直都很安静。

paragraphs.en: As a flight attendant assists him down the tunnel, I wave. Carl scowls.
paragraphs.zh: 一名空乘人员在隧道里扶着他下去时，我向他挥了挥手。卡尔则是皱着眉。

paragraphs.en: Evelyn, the woman I wheel to her Taipei flight, speaks gently. I don’t understand Mandarin. But I still hum at the breaks in her speech, and nod when she glances my way. She has a sash around her neck, and she asks me to feel its edges. Its pastel corners are thinning. Evelyn presses the fabric against her cheeks.
paragraphs.zh: Evelyn是我推着去乘台北航班的那位女性。她说话很轻，但我听不懂普通话。不过我仍会在她停顿的地方轻轻回应，并在她朝我看一眼时点头。她脖子上围着一条披肩，让我去摸它的边缘。淡彩的边角已经变薄了。Evelyn把那块布贴在脸颊上。

paragraphs.en: When I drop her off at the gate, a chubby Taiwanese flight attendant asks what we talk about.
paragraphs.zh: 我把她送到登机口时，一位体态圆润的台湾空乘问我们都聊了些什么。

paragraphs.en: We have an understanding, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，我们心照不宣。

paragraphs.en: Bullshit, he says. I can barely make out her accent.
paragraphs.zh: 他嘲笑道：胡扯。我几乎听不出她的口音。

paragraphs.en: O.K., O.K., I say. Then we’re kindred spirits.
paragraphs.zh: “好吧，好吧。”我说，“那我们可算是志同道合的人了。”

paragraphs.en: The flight attendant gives me a look. When he takes Evelyn’s arm, she’s still rubbing the sash between her fingers.
paragraphs.zh: 空乘瞥了我一眼。当他扶着伊芙琳的胳膊时，她还在用手指摩挲着她的丝带。

paragraphs.en: Sometimes I accompany mixed groups. When I walk a lady to her New Orleans flight, she’s with two friends, all in Saints jerseys, sailor caps, thigh-high boots. Laughing. Yelling. Other passengers glide around us. The ladies ask me to stop at a sports bar ten minutes from their gate. When I warn them about the time, they insist that the plane will wait.
paragraphs.zh: 有时我会陪同一些混合组团。当我陪一位女士去新奥尔良的航班时，她和两个朋友同行，三人都穿着圣徒队球衣、海员帽和齐膝靴。她们笑个不停，大声喊叫。其他乘客从我们身边轻盈地走过。女士们让我在离登机口十分钟路程的体育酒吧停一下。我提醒她们时间不多，她们却坚持说飞机会等。

paragraphs.en: Podcast: The Writer’s Voice Listen to Bryan Washington read “Gatekeeping.”
paragraphs.zh: 播客：《作家之声》：收听 Bryan Washington 朗读《把关》。

paragraphs.en: The woman I’ve been wheeling, Rose, wears gargantuan glasses. Three times the size of my own. After one margarita, and then again after her third, she asks if I have a wife.
paragraphs.zh: 我扶着的那个女人，罗斯，戴着巨大无比的眼镜，是我自己的三倍大。第一次喝玛格丽塔酒后，又在她第三杯酒后，她问我是否有妻子。

paragraphs.en: Must, Anne says. Looks like you eat well enough.
paragraphs.zh: “可以的，”安妮说，“看起来你吃得也挺不错。”

paragraphs.en: Then a husband, Gladys says.
paragraphs.zh: 然后是丈夫，格拉迪斯说。

paragraphs.en: Listen, Rose says. Do me a favor? If you want kids, have them now. Or don’t have them at all. You’ll just be a burden when you’re older. I’m telling you the truth.
paragraphs.zh: “听着，”罗斯说，“给我一个帮忙，好吗？如果你想要孩子，现在就生。或者干脆别生。等你老了，你只会成累赘。我跟你说这话是实话。”

paragraphs.en: To God’s ears, Gladys says.
paragraphs.zh: 格拉迪丝说：‘听着上帝的话。’

paragraphs.en: Then Rose taps me on the shoulder. She asks about my favorite N.F.L. team, and I inform her that I don’t have one.
paragraphs.zh: 然后罗丝拍了拍我的肩膀。她问我最喜欢的 N.F.L. 球队是哪支，我告诉她我没有固定支持的球队。

paragraphs.en: Rose runs a finger across the collar of her jersey, cackling, and yells, Me neither!
paragraphs.zh: 罗丝用手指抚过球衣领口，咯咯笑着，大声说：‘我也是！’

paragraphs.en: Sometimes I’m the man in the middle. A white couple I’m assisting to their Berlin flight—the husband’s in the wheelchair—snipe at each other, in hushed voices, for our entire walk to the gate.
paragraphs.zh: 有时我就成了中间人。我陪一对白人夫妇去柏林航班——丈夫坐着轮椅——他们在我们走向登机口的整段路上，都在低声互相讥讽。

paragraphs.en: You probably left the stove on, the wife says.
paragraphs.zh: 你肯定把炉子没关，妻子说。

paragraphs.en: The better to burn your son’s house down, the husband says.
paragraphs.zh: 更好的是为了把你儿子家的房子烧掉，丈夫说。

paragraphs.en: Leave your jacket on the security tray, he adds. It’s a rag.
paragraphs.zh: 把你的外套放在安检托盘上吧，他补充道。那是一块抹布。

paragraphs.en: I didn’t want you to feel left out, the wife says.
paragraphs.zh: 我不想让你有被排斥在外的感觉，妻子说。

paragraphs.en: Don’t bother helping him out of the chair, she adds, to me, as I lead him to the metal detector. Give him something to actually do.
paragraphs.zh: 她对我说：不用费心帮他从椅子上站起来，我把他带到金属探测器那边时。给他一件真正能做的事吧。

paragraphs.en: I smile, nodding. I am a rock and a sponge. When we finally make it to the gate, I pass the husband off to a flight attendant, an Indian twink who throws me a knowing look. But the wife stands beside me.
paragraphs.zh: 我微笑着点了点头。我在这种时候既沉默又耐心。当我们终于走到登机口时，我把丈夫交给了一名空乘，一位年轻俊俏的印度男乘务员，他朝我投来了会意的一笑。可妻子仍然站在我身边。

paragraphs.en: What if I just stay here? she says.
paragraphs.zh: 如果我就留在这儿呢？她说。

paragraphs.en: It is a question without an answer. And I don’t know if she wants one. So I loom beside her in silence, until last call sounds and she sighs and boards the flight.
paragraphs.zh: 这问题没有答案，而且我不知道她是否想要答案。所以在最后一次呼叫响起之前，我都沉默地站在她身边，直到她叹了口气，随后登上了飞机。

paragraphs.en: That night, the chubby Taiwanese flight attendant—JT—finally comes on my belly, and I do, too. Then I roll onto my side. We’re silent except for the television’s drone, and then he chuckles, exhaling. Some women in the hotel room next door are throwing a bachelorette party.
paragraphs.zh: 那天晚上，那个圆滚滚的台湾空乘——JT——终于骑到了我的肚子上，我也随之坐了上去。然后我转向一侧。除了电视机持续的嗡鸣声，我们都没说话，接着他轻笑着呼出一口气。隔壁酒店房间里有几个女人在举办新娘出发前的派对。

paragraphs.en: I said it’s cool to finish inside me, JT says.
paragraphs.zh: 他说，在我里面结束也可以。

paragraphs.en: I don’t know, I say. Feels weird.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，我不知道。感觉怪怪的。

paragraphs.en: Suit yourself, JT says, reaching for his glasses.
paragraphs.zh: JT说，随你吧，伸手去拿他的眼镜。

paragraphs.en: Both of us tap at our phones. Michael B. Jordan hits a punching bag in front of us, yelling about somebody’s legacy.
paragraphs.zh: 我们两个都在摸着手机。迈克尔·B·乔丹在我们前面打着沙袋，大声咆哮着某人的“遗产”问题。

paragraphs.en: It’s because of your face, JT says. That’s why they talk to you that way.
paragraphs.zh: JT说，这是因为你的脸。你们才会那样跟你说话。

paragraphs.en: You think so? I ask.
paragraphs.zh: 你也这么想？我问道。

paragraphs.en: Soft features. Everyone’s always touching you, right?
paragraphs.zh: 你那张柔和的五官。每个人都总是在碰你，对吧？

paragraphs.en: Fat-people problems. Happens to me, too.
paragraphs.zh: “胖人问题。”我也有同感。

paragraphs.en: It’s JT’s second night in the hotel. Some kind of flight delay. Which makes this our first back-to-back in months. We’ve been doing whatever this is for about two years now. The coffee table’s covered with mostly unfinished takeout noodles, because JT ate only a few bites before deeming them inedible.
paragraphs.zh: 这是 JT 在这家酒店的第二个晚上。又是某种航班延误。这让我们几个月来第一次连着住在一起。我们做这行已经有两年了。咖啡桌上几乎全是还没吃完的外卖面，因为 JT 只吃了几口就觉得不卫生可口就不再碰了。

paragraphs.en: Now he reaches for his vape and takes a pull. When he offers it to me, I inhale a slower one.
paragraphs.zh: 这时他拿起电子烟吸了一口。轮到他递给我时，我就吸得慢一点。

paragraphs.en: Once, JT says, this pilot spent our entire shift talking shit. Just punching down. I couldn’t do anything right. When the other attendants tried intervening, that didn’t help at all. But after we landed I caught him waiting for me on the bridge.
paragraphs.zh: 有一次，JT 说，那位飞行员整整在我们值机班次里满口粗话、总在贬低人。那时我什么也做不对。其他乘务员试着介入也完全没用。但在我们降落后，我在走道上把他拦了下来。

paragraphs.en: To finish you off, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我要说完了。

paragraphs.en: Right? You’d think. He had this look on his face, like he was so fucking embarrassed. Then he puts his hand on my side and asks if I want to get dinner.
paragraphs.zh: 对吧？你会这么想。他的脸上带着一种表情，像是他非常羞愧得不行。然后他把手搭在我侧边，问我想不想去吃晚饭。

paragraphs.en: JT sets a palm on my belly. Our cum’s still on it. When I make a face, he rolls his eyes, wiping some of the mess on his stomach, too.
paragraphs.zh: JT把手掌放在我肚子上。我们的精液还留在上面。我一做出不满的表情，他翻了个白眼，也把一些脏东西擦到了他的肚子上。

paragraphs.en: So, I say, what happened?
paragraphs.zh: 于是我问：到底发生了什么？

paragraphs.en: What do you think? JT says. I told him to go fuck himself.
paragraphs.zh: “你怎么想？”JT说。 我回他说：去他妈的。

paragraphs.en: Wrong answer, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说：这答案不对。

paragraphs.en: Yeah. You could’ve been a househusband by now.
paragraphs.zh: 是啊。你现在都能当个全职家庭主夫了。

paragraphs.en: I like working, JT says. That’s my problem. Yours, too.
paragraphs.zh: JT说：我喜欢工作。这是我的问题。你也一样。

paragraphs.en: Then he squeezes my hip, sticking out his tongue.
paragraphs.zh: 然后他捏了捏我的臀部，又把舌头伸了出来。

paragraphs.en: The next morning, JT starts getting ready to leave while I’m still in bed, but eventually I get up and pack my shit alongside him. In the elevator, he says that if I just came to Taipei, then I wouldn’t have to wake up so early.
paragraphs.zh: 第二天早上，JT在我还躺在床上时就开始收拾准备离开，后来我还是起床了，和他一起把东西打包好。在电梯里他对我说，如果我刚到台北，我就不用这么早起床。

paragraphs.en: My sister owns a coffee shop below our apartment, he says. We’d walk right downstairs for breakfast. Danbing and luo buo gao.
paragraphs.zh: 他说，我姐姐在我们公寓楼下开了一家咖啡店。我们会直接下楼去吃早餐，蛋饼和萝卜糕。

paragraphs.en: We’ll practice that before you get there.
paragraphs.zh: 你到那边之前，我们先练一练。

paragraphs.en: I couldn’t even find Taiwan on a map, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，我连地图上都找不到台湾。

paragraphs.en: Look for China and poke around, JT says, and snorts, hopping into an airport shuttle.
paragraphs.zh: JT说：先找找中国再四处逛逛，边说边“呲”了一声，然后跳上了机场摆渡车。

paragraphs.en: So I drive home. My place is twenty minutes from the airport. And my brother’s on the sofa, because he is always on the sofa. When I lock the door, he yells my name from the living room.
paragraphs.zh: 所以我开车回家。我的住处离机场二十分钟路程。我的哥哥正坐在沙发上，因为他总是坐在沙发上。我一锁上门，他就在客厅里叫我的名字。

paragraphs.en: He’s playing video games, smoking weed. Two of his friends occupy the cushions around him. They nod at me and I nod at them, and I ask my brother if he’s eaten and he tells me there’s no food.
paragraphs.zh: 他在打电子游戏，还在抽大麻。两个朋友坐在他周围的软垫上。他们朝我点头，我也朝他们点头，然后我问我弟弟他有没有吃饭，他告诉我已经没有吃的了。

paragraphs.en: I bought groceries yesterday, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，我昨天买了杂货。

paragraphs.en: But there’s no food, my brother says.
paragraphs.zh: 但他说，冰箱里什么都没有。

paragraphs.en: Right. I’ll make sandwiches or something.
paragraphs.zh: 对。那我就做个三明治之类的吧。

paragraphs.en: Word, my brother says, not looking away from the screen.
paragraphs.zh: “我懂了，”我哥哥说着，目光始终没离开屏幕。

paragraphs.en: But instead of stepping into the kitchen I slump toward my room and lock the door behind me. I strip, lie down on the carpet, and spread my arms and legs, fanning.
paragraphs.zh: 不过我没有走进厨房，而是朝自己的房间沉重地挪去，在身后把门锁上。我脱下衣服，躺在地毯上，双臂双腿张开，像扇子一样摊开。

paragraphs.en: That’s something I picked up. My first month on the job, I walked a white woman to her midday Vancouver flight. Sarah was entirely pleasant, and then she informed me that she had several months left to live.
paragraphs.zh: 这是我学会的一个动作。入职的第一个月，我曾陪一位白人女性走到她前往温哥华的中午航班。萨拉人很好，然后她告诉我，她还活着的时间只剩下几个月了。

paragraphs.en: Four or five, she said, smiling. Give or take.
paragraphs.zh: 她说大概四五个月，笑着说的，前后差不多。

paragraphs.en: Terminal diagnosis. So be gentle, dear.
paragraphs.zh: 是末期诊断。要温柔一点，亲爱的。

paragraphs.en: I’d only just finished orientation. Hadn’t seen this situation in the manual. I was silent as I pushed her wheelchair through security, then immigration, before she asked if I could wheel her to the terrace. Once we were outside, Sarah gripped my wrist and stood, then lay down on the cool tile. Then she spread her arms and legs. Slowly, she brushed them along the ground.
paragraphs.zh: 我刚刚结束了新手训练，手册里完全没写过这种情况。我把她的轮椅推过安检和移民区时一直沉默。然后她问我能不能把她推到露台上。我们一到外面，莎拉握住我的手腕，先是站起来，然后躺在凉凉的瓷砖上。接着她张开了手臂和腿，缓慢地把它们沿着地面擦过。

paragraphs.en: Want to try? Sarah asked.
paragraphs.zh: 想试试吗？莎拉问。

paragraphs.en: I did not. The air was too crisp. But I lay down beside her. Fanning slowly, until our movements matched, and then our breathing, too.
paragraphs.zh: 我没有尝试。空气太冷了。可我还是躺在她身旁，慢慢地摆动，直到我们的动作对齐，然后连呼吸也对齐了。

paragraphs.en: Sometimes I do this, Sarah said, to remind myself that I’m still here. I won’t be, eventually. And that’s O.K. Because it has to be. But, right now, I am.
paragraphs.zh: 莎拉说，她有时会这样做，为了提醒自己自己还在这里。她终究不会一直待在这里，而那是可以的，因为必须如此。只是现在，她还在。

paragraphs.en: Then she sighed, deeply, pushing all the air out of her body.
paragraphs.zh: 随后她深深地叹了一口气，把体内的全部空气都呼出。

paragraphs.en: This week, Carl stares ahead in silence for our entire walk to the gate. No quips about passersby (pajamas in the airport, grown fucking toddlers), or the shouting from random children (Neanderthals breeding Neanderthals), or the pop music droning overhead (fentanyl). He’s just quiet. When we stop for coffee, Carl’s still wordless—he doesn’t send his order back or switch the milk at the last minute or berate the barista in French.
paragraphs.zh: 这周，在我们走到大门的整段路上，卡尔一直沉默地望着前方。既没有对过路人开玩笑（机场里的睡衣、放肆的幼儿），也没有对随机小孩的叫喊发牢骚（尼安德特人正在繁衍尼安德特人），也没有听见头顶持续轰鸣的流行音乐（《Fentanyl》）。他就是很安静。我们停下来买咖啡时，卡尔仍旧一句话也不说——既不喊他点的东西，也不临时换牛奶，更不会用法语骂咖啡师。

paragraphs.en: When I ask what’s wrong, Carl gives me a long look. Then he clears his throat.
paragraphs.zh: 我问他怎么了，卡尔抬眼看了我很久。然后他清了清嗓子。

paragraphs.en: I’m going to the hospital, he says. My oldest friend in this world is sick. Tumor in his head.
paragraphs.zh: “我要去医院，”他说，“我在这个世界上最老的朋友病了。头上长了个肿瘤。”

paragraphs.en: Oh, I say. I’m sorry to—
paragraphs.zh: 哦，我说。真的很抱歉——

paragraphs.en: Stop, Carl says. You were doing just fine.
paragraphs.zh: 停，卡尔说。你做得很好。

paragraphs.en: Then he looks down at his hands.
paragraphs.zh: 然后他低头看向自己的双手。

paragraphs.en: He and I didn’t speak for two years, Carl says. Over a woman. Not just her but, you know. He spends one fucking summer in Kanazawa, and all of a sudden she’s his wife. We were thirty, thirty-one. I’m their daughter’s godfather. Do you know I learned fucking Japanese to speak to this fucking child? The girl’s a doctor now, and she doesn’t listen to anyone, and she’s perfect, and her mom is, too. But I wasted two years not speaking to my friend.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔说，他和我断了两年联系。因为一个女人。不是只有她一个人，你懂的。他在金泽只住了一个他妈的夏天，忽然她就成了他的妻子。我们那时三十岁、三十一岁。我是他们女儿的教父。你知道我为了和这个他妈的孩子说话而学了他妈的日语吗？现在那个女孩是医生，而且谁也不听，她很完美，她妈妈也一样。可我却浪费了两年没有和我的朋友说话。

paragraphs.en: Carl’s silent again, and then he sneezes. I hold out a handkerchief. A whole minute passes before he accepts it.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔又沉默了片刻，然后他打了个喷嚏。我递给他一方手帕。他接过之前，足足过了一分钟。

paragraphs.en: When we reach the gate, I ask for his friend’s name. He gives me a long look.
paragraphs.zh: 当我们到达登机口时，我问他朋友的名字。他看了我很久。

paragraphs.en: So you’re a fucking brain surgeon now? he asks.
paragraphs.zh: 他问道：你现在他妈是个脑外科医生了？

paragraphs.en: Later, I walk a woman to her Mexico City flight. Her daughter passes her off to me after check-in.
paragraphs.zh: 后来，我把一位女士送去她前往墨西哥城的航班。她的女儿在办理登机手续后把她交给了我。

paragraphs.en: Watch my mother’s hands, she warns me.
paragraphs.zh: 她警告我：盯着我妈妈的手。

paragraphs.en: I’ll do my best, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说：我会尽力的。

paragraphs.en: I’m serious, the daughter says. They wander.
paragraphs.zh: 女儿说：我是认真的。她们在乱动。

paragraphs.en: The woman, Catalina, asks a question in Spanish, and the daughter replies nada something or other before her phone rings, and she gives me a frown, and then she’s gone.
paragraphs.zh: 那个叫卡塔莉娜的女人用西班牙语问了个问题，女儿在她的电话响前随口回了个“nada”之类的词，然后对我皱了皱眉，接着就走了。

paragraphs.en: As I walk Catalina through the airport, she crochets what looks like a ball. Her hands move deftly around a hook. From time to time, she holds the ball up for me to inspect. It is gorgeous, split between bright colors and soft pastels.
paragraphs.zh: 当我陪Catalina走过机场时，她一边走一边用钩针编了一个像球一样的东西。她的手在钩针上灵巧地穿梭。她不时把那个球举起来让我看看。它非常漂亮，色彩明快又带着柔和的粉彩感。

paragraphs.en: A toddler chewing pretzels and his sister amble through security beside us. Catalina holds the ball up for them to see. The T.S.A. agents turn the sphere in their hands, and ask if she sells them. Catalina just keeps at it, as we pass from gate to gate, until we reach her flight.
paragraphs.zh: 一个边啃椒盐脆饼、和他妹妹并肩在安检旁慢慢走过来的幼儿园小孩。Catalina把球举起来让他们看。T.S.A.工作人员把球在手里转了转，并问她是否在出售这些。Catalina只是持续地做着，直到我们从一个登机口走到下一个登机口，最终到达她的登机口。

paragraphs.en: That’s when I see the bar of chocolate in her pocket.
paragraphs.zh: 就在那时，我看见她口袋里那块巧克力。

paragraphs.en: And that toddler’s pretzels in her tote bag.
paragraphs.zh: 还有那位幼儿园小孩在她的购物袋里啃着的椒盐脆饼。

paragraphs.en: Three water bottles and a baseball cap are tucked underneath them.
paragraphs.zh: 三瓶水和一顶棒球帽被塞在他们身下。

paragraphs.en: Catalina follows my eyes. She sets one finger to her lips, winking as the flight attendant wheels her away.
paragraphs.zh: 卡特琳娜顺着我的目光看去。她一根手指按在嘴唇上，向我眨眼，当空乘把她推着送走时。

paragraphs.en: Later, I’m taking a guy named Edwin to his Montego Bay flight. One of my co-workers, José, comes along, because he swears he has nothing better to do. But I’m grateful: Edwin speaks in a rapid-fire patois. José nods along, clapping his hands.
paragraphs.zh: 后来，我送一位叫埃德温的家伙去他去蒙特哥贝的航班。我的一位同事何塞也一起过来，因为他发誓说自己没别的事可做。不过我很感激：埃德温说的是飞快的克里奥尔混杂语。何塞一边点头一边拍掌。

paragraphs.en: When we reach Edwin’s gate, José waves us off, and when the two of us are alone the old man finally turns to me. Beckons me forward, as if he has something to whisper. Then he kisses me on the cheek.
paragraphs.zh: 当我们到达埃德温的登机口时，何塞把我们放开，当我们两人独处时，这位老人终于朝我转过身来。像要悄悄对我说什么似的，把我招了过来。然后他在我脸颊上轻轻吻了一下。

paragraphs.en: I pull away. Edwin just stares, a goofy smile on his face, before he’s whisked onto his flight.
paragraphs.zh: 我后退了一步。埃德文只是站着，脸上带着傻乎乎的笑容，然后就被带上了他的航班。

paragraphs.en: Afterward, walking through the terminal, José asks what happened.
paragraphs.zh: 之后，我在机场走廊里走着，何塞问我发生了什么。

paragraphs.en: When I tell him, he squints.
paragraphs.zh: 当我把事情告诉他时，他眯起了眼。

paragraphs.en: Oh, he says, that’s assault.
paragraphs.zh: “哦，”他说，“那就是性侵犯。”

paragraphs.en: Fuck that. Old fucking bag.
paragraphs.zh: 去他妈的。那老该死的老东西。

paragraphs.en: They’re already airborne. It can’t be helped.
paragraphs.zh: 他们已经在空中了，已经没办法了。

paragraphs.en: José fumes. Then he cracks a grin, just slightly.
paragraphs.zh: 何塞恼火地皱起了眉头，随后嘴角仅仅微微上扬了一下。

paragraphs.en: He was telling me about his wife, José says, and how he couldn’t wait to get back to her. But he was also trying to persuade me to visit him in Jamaica? Said he’d pay for everything?
paragraphs.zh: 他跟我说起了自己的妻子，说他迫不及待想要回去见她。可他也在劝我去牙买加看他？他还说他会付一切费用。

paragraphs.en: Fuck you, José says.
paragraphs.zh: “去你妈的，”何塞说。

paragraphs.en: Then he chuckles, shrugging.
paragraphs.zh: 然后他笑了笑，耸了耸肩。

paragraphs.en: But I guess you really never know, he says, right?
paragraphs.zh: 但我想，你也许真不知道，对吧？”他这样说。

paragraphs.en: A few nights later, when I tell JT about the incident, he gives me a long look.
paragraphs.zh: 几天后，我把这起事件告诉JT时，他给了我一个很久的凝视。

paragraphs.en: My brother is closeted, he says.
paragraphs.zh: 他说，他弟弟是个没出柜的人。

paragraphs.en: Yeah. Married with kids in Taichung. His wife is great. But, you know.
paragraphs.zh: 是啊。他在台中有妻子和孩子。她人很好。只是，你懂的。

paragraphs.en: We’re in JT’s hotel room again. Lounging in boxers with our legs wrapped around each other. He’s got a week off for the first time in a while, and he has declared that he wants to spend it doing absolutely nothing.
paragraphs.zh: 我们又在JT的酒店房间了。两个人都只穿着内裤，腿紧紧地盘在一起懒散地躺着。他这第一次在一段时间里有一整周假期，于是他宣布要把这周全部用来什么都不做。

paragraphs.en: So I did something I never do: called in sick. My manager, a Colombian woman who could be twenty-five or fifty-two, agreed immediately.
paragraphs.zh: 所以我做了一件从未做过的事：请了病假。我的经理是个哥伦比亚女人，看起来像25岁也像52岁，她立刻答应了。

paragraphs.en: Do you think that old fucker knew you were queer? JT asks.
paragraphs.zh: 你觉得那老家伙知道你是同志吗？JT问道。

paragraphs.en: Did you know I was queer? I ask.
paragraphs.zh: 你知道我是什么性向吗？我问。

paragraphs.en: Are you being funny?
paragraphs.zh: 你在逗我玩吗？

paragraphs.en: Don’t insult me like that again.
paragraphs.zh: 别再那样侮辱我。

paragraphs.en: Fine. How did you find out about your brother?
paragraphs.zh: 好的，你是怎么知道你哥哥的事的？

paragraphs.en: JT blinks for a while. Then he sighs.
paragraphs.zh: JT 眨了一会儿眼，然后叹了口气。

paragraphs.en: He had a boyfriend, JT says. The dude worked at our post office. They were together for years, but then he broke up with my brother. And then, you know, they both got married to family friends.
paragraphs.zh: 他说他有一个男朋友。那人是在我们邮局上班。两个人交往了很多年，后来他和我兄弟分手了。然后，你知道吗，他们后来都嫁给了家庭中的朋友。

paragraphs.en: We lie there for a while, the television playing in front of us.
paragraphs.zh: 我们就那样躺着一会儿，电视在我们面前放映着。

paragraphs.en: You live with your brother, JT says.
paragraphs.zh: 你和你弟弟，JT 说。

paragraphs.en: Then he can’t afford his own place?
paragraphs.zh: 那他可买不起自己的住处吗？

paragraphs.en: He gambles on his phone, I say. Or something. But he probably makes more money than me. He went to cooking school for a while.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，他在手机上赌博，或者是做点别的。但他可能比我挣得更多。他以前还去过一段时间厨师学校。

paragraphs.en: You’ve got a lot of questions tonight.
paragraphs.zh: 你今晚问了好多问题。

paragraphs.en: Look, I say, it’s just better for me to be around.
paragraphs.zh: 我想说，还是我待在这里更合适。

paragraphs.en: For him, JT says, or for you?
paragraphs.zh: JT说：是对他有好处，还是对你有好处？

paragraphs.en: You really are a cop.
paragraphs.zh: 你真的是警察。

paragraphs.en: Just due diligence, JT says. And I still need time to recharge.
paragraphs.zh: 这只是尽到必要的谨慎，JT说。我现在还需要时间来恢复精力。

paragraphs.en: We’re not fucking again.
paragraphs.zh: 我们不会再和你发生性关系了。

paragraphs.en: That’s what you said last week, JT says. So it’s a guilt thing? With your brother.
paragraphs.zh: JT说你上周也是这么说的。那么这是因为什么内疚吗？和你兄弟有关？

paragraphs.en: What makes you say that?
paragraphs.zh: 你为什么会这么说？

paragraphs.en: Guilt smells a certain way.
paragraphs.zh: 内疚有一种特别的气味。

paragraphs.en: He has a panic disorder, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 他有惊恐症，我说。

paragraphs.en: Yeah. If I leave him, I don’t know what’ll happen. He’s done a lot for me, though.
paragraphs.zh: 是啊。如果我离开他，我不知道会发生什么。不过他也为我做了很多。

paragraphs.en: JT gives me a long look. I feel myself swallowing spit.
paragraphs.zh: JT看着我，目光很久不移。我感觉自己咽了口口水。

paragraphs.en: But you know what else smells? I say, leaning toward JT, running my tongue across his chest and making my way down, and I know he has more questions, but when I take his dick into my mouth there is nothing left to say.
paragraphs.zh: 但你知道还有什么味道吗？我说着靠向JT，用舌头在他的胸口轻扫，再往下滑，我知道他还有更多问题想问，但当我把他的鸡巴含进嘴里时，已经没什么话可说了。

paragraphs.en: A woman I’m walking to her Taipei flight is stopped for a random security check. Her husband, Van, stands beside us. The security officer—a dumpy white guy—asks Linh to hand over her bag for screening. He asks once, and then again, before over-enunciating a third time.
paragraphs.zh: 我陪一位要去台北航班的妇女走着，结果她被叫去随机安检。她的丈夫范站在我们旁边。一个身材矮胖的白人安全员让Linh把包交出来接受检查。他先问了一次，又问了一次，第三次时还故意把每个字都咬得很重。

paragraphs.en: She can’t speak English, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说她不会说英语。

paragraphs.en: You think I don’t fucking know that? the officer says.
paragraphs.zh: 那名安全员说：你以为我他妈不知道吗？

paragraphs.en: He tries again, staring at Linh, until Van turns to me. Throws up his hands.
paragraphs.zh: 他又试了一次，并一直盯着Linh看，直到范转向我，双手举了起来。

paragraphs.en: Look, I say, this woman’s been through security already. There’s no problem.
paragraphs.zh: 看，她已经过了安检了，没问题。

paragraphs.en: That’s not up to you, the officer says.
paragraphs.zh: 这不是你说了算的，警官说道。

paragraphs.en: He reaches for Linh’s bag, tugging. She takes hold of the other end. But when she turns to me I can only wince and shrug.
paragraphs.zh: 他伸手去拽琳的包向自己这边拉，她抓住了另一端。但是当她转向我时，我只能眯了下眼并耸了耸肩。

paragraphs.en: Then she turns to Van. He nods, just once. His eyes never leave the security officer.
paragraphs.zh: 然后她转向文。他只点了下头，一次而已。

paragraphs.en: So the officer opens her bag and rifles through it. Shakes his head, extracting photographs. They’re old. Full of family. The officer flips through them, fingering Polaroids of buildings, children, what looks like a young couple, and a bunch of gatherings.
paragraphs.zh: 于是警官打开了她的包并狠狠翻找起来。她摇了摇头，掏出一叠照片。那些是旧照片，都是家庭相关的。警官翻看着它们，用手指掂着那些宝丽来：有建筑、有孩子、一个看起来像年轻夫妇的画面，还有许多聚会的合影。

paragraphs.en: They’re fucking pictures, the officer says. Why didn’t you just fucking say that?
paragraphs.zh: “这些都是下流的照片，”警官说，“你为什么一开始就直接说呢？”

paragraphs.en: Because she can’t speak English, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 因为她不会说英语，我说。

paragraphs.en: Did I fucking ask you? Are you the one that I’m talking to?
paragraphs.zh: “我他妈在问你吗？你才是我在对你说话的人吗？”

paragraphs.en: You don’t have literally anything else to do?
paragraphs.zh: 你难道真没别的事可做了吗？

paragraphs.en: The officer glares at me. Harder. There’s something he wants to say. But the walkie-talkie on his waist buzzes.
paragraphs.zh: 警官盯着我。更用力地。似乎有话想对我说。可他腰间的对讲机开始嗡嗡作响。

paragraphs.en: He reaches for it, grunting as he walks off.
paragraphs.zh: 他伸手去拿，对着走远的动作低低哼了一声。

paragraphs.en: Van and I stand in silence. Then we both turn to Linh. She watches the officer until he’s lost in the crowd and then, resolutely, throws up two middle fingers.
paragraphs.zh: Van 和我默默站着。然后我们都转向 Linh。她一直盯着警官，直到他消失在人群里，然后干脆利落地比出两个中指。

paragraphs.en: It’s storming outside when I jog to a Toronto flight’s gate. I’ve been called over to help with a passenger’s move, which is rare after someone’s already been taken to their gate. The rain casts sheets of water across the terminal’s windows. Travellers press their noses against the glass.
paragraphs.zh: 我跑向多伦多航班登机口时，外面正下着大风暴。有人叫我过去帮一位乘客搬东西，这在乘客已经被带到登机口后是很少见的。雨水像水帘一样泼洒在航站楼的窗户上。旅客把鼻子贴在玻璃上。

paragraphs.en: I see the woman in her chair. Also her son. When I ask him where their gate is, he grins, pantomiming.
paragraphs.zh: 我看到那位坐在轮椅上的女士，也看到了她的儿子。当我问他他们的登机口在哪儿时，他咧嘴一笑，并做了个动作示意。

paragraphs.en: So I text JT. I know he’s working today, busy with a flight back to Taipei, but it’s only a few moments before he materializes beside me—and then the four of us are walking together.
paragraphs.zh: 于是我给JT发了条短信。我知道他今天在值班，忙着处理一班飞往台北的航班，但没过多久他就出现在我身边——然后我们四个人一起走了出去。

paragraphs.en: The son holds his mother’s hand, whispering into her ear. I push the woman along, as our group weaves through the crowd. From time to time, he points and she smiles. Sometimes she turns my way, as if she isn’t entirely sure why I’m there.
paragraphs.zh: 儿子握着妈妈的手，贴着她耳边低声说着。我们一行人在拥挤的人群中穿梭，我推着这位女士前行。时不时，他会指一下，她就会笑。她有时会转向我这边，仿佛还没有完全确定我在这里的原因。

paragraphs.en: Eventually, we make it to the gate, and I ask JT why they even requested me.
paragraphs.zh: 最终，我们走到了门口，我问 JT 为什么他们会让我来。

paragraphs.en: JT talks to the son in Mandarin. The son looks offended. But then his face softens, and his voice is lighter than I expected.
paragraphs.zh: JT 用普通话和儿子说了几句。儿子看起来有些不快，但随后表情缓和了，声音也比我预想的轻快。

paragraphs.en: These two didn’t, JT says. Her actual son did. From Toronto.
paragraphs.zh: 这两个不是，JT 说。她真正的儿子是有的，来自多伦多。

paragraphs.en: Yeah, JT says. He wouldn’t have known about the sugar baby. Obviously.
paragraphs.zh: 是啊，JT 说。他当然不会知道那位“sugar baby”的事。

paragraphs.en: The woman watches out the window as planes ascend and descend. Then there’s the chime of a gate announcement. The sugar baby nods at me, once, and the woman smiles as they make their way toward the flight. Which leaves JT and me standing beside each other, not really saying anything.
paragraphs.zh: 那位女人望着窗外，飞机在起飞和降落。接着传来了一声登机口广播。那位糖宝对我点了点头，女人则对我们走向登机口的过程报以微笑。这样一来，JT和我就在一旁并肩站着，没有真正说一句话。

paragraphs.en: We brush shoulders. Neither of us acknowledges it. But we don’t move, either. We wait until the plane takes off, lingering a little after it’s gone.
paragraphs.zh: 我们肩膀轻轻碰了一下。我们谁都没有承认。可我们也都没有离开。我们等着飞机起飞，在它离去后又停留了一会儿。

paragraphs.en: That night, we’re smoking on the roof of JT’s hotel.
paragraphs.zh: 那天晚上，我们在JT的酒店屋顶上吸烟。

paragraphs.en: How come you’ve never brought me to your place? he says.
paragraphs.zh: 你怎么从来没把我带去过你家？他问道。

paragraphs.en: Nothing to see, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说：没什么好看的。

paragraphs.en: JT snorts. We’re both in tank tops and sweatpants. I’m borrowing his clothes, which fit me nearly perfectly.
paragraphs.zh: JT哼了一声。我们都穿着背心和运动裤。我的衣服是他借给我的，几乎完全合适。

paragraphs.en: There’s always something to see, JT says. I could learn more about you.
paragraphs.zh: JT说，总有点什么值得看。我还可以更多地了解你。

paragraphs.en: I think you know plenty about me.
paragraphs.zh: 我想你对我其实已经很了解了。

paragraphs.en: You could be a father of five.
paragraphs.zh: 你可能是个五个孩子的父亲。

paragraphs.en: Are you even Taiwanese?
paragraphs.zh: 你到底算不算台湾人？

paragraphs.en: But I’m serious, I say. I’m an open book.
paragraphs.zh: 但我不是在开玩笑，我说。我是完全公开透明的。

paragraphs.en: Really, I say. Ask away.
paragraphs.zh: 真心的，我是说的。尽管问吧。

paragraphs.en: Seeing with my own eyes is better, JT says.
paragraphs.zh: JT说，亲眼看到才更靠谱。

paragraphs.en: Just moments before, we fucked on his bed. After he finished in my mouth, I leaned in for a kiss, and he kissed me back. Afterward, while washing each other in the shower, he told me he’d never done that with anyone else before.
paragraphs.zh: 就在刚才，我们在他的床上做爱了。他射在我嘴里后，我凑过去想亲他，他也回了个吻。之后我们在淋浴里互相洗着澡时，他说他以前从没和其他任何人做过这种事。

paragraphs.en: Fine, JT said. Just one other guy.
paragraphs.zh: “好吧，”JT说，“只做过一个别的男人。”

paragraphs.en: Stop. You’re one of the handful. A rare mineral.
paragraphs.zh: 停下。你是那为数不多的人之一。很罕见的一种矿物。

paragraphs.en: Now, on the roof, he nudges me. We hear planes taking off into the evening sky, but we can’t see them.
paragraphs.zh: 现在，在屋顶上，他轻轻推了我一下。我们听见飞机升空飞向晚霞中的天空，但我们看不见它们。

paragraphs.en: If you could travel anywhere, I ask, where would you go?
paragraphs.zh: 我问他：如果你可以去任何地方，你会去哪里？

paragraphs.en: I can travel anywhere, JT says. That is the point of my job.
paragraphs.zh: JT说：我能去任何地方。这就是我工作的意义。

paragraphs.en: JT scratches his ass. Then he smiles.
paragraphs.zh: JT挠了挠屁股。然后他笑了。

paragraphs.en: Yeah. I dated a Palestinian guy for a little while. You?
paragraphs.zh: 是的。我和一个巴勒斯坦男生谈过一阵子。你呢？

paragraphs.en: I don’t know, I say. It’s been a while since I left Houston. Amsterdam? Oaxaca? Osaka?
paragraphs.zh: 我说我不知道。我离开休斯敦已经有一阵子了。阿姆斯特丹？瓦哈卡？大阪？

paragraphs.en: If I go, then I’ll let you know.
paragraphs.zh: 如果我去的话，会告诉你。

paragraphs.en: Try Tokyo first, JT says. When you’re done being bullied for not speaking Japanese, pit-stop at my place.
paragraphs.zh: 先去东京吧，JT 说。等你不再因为不会说日语被人欺负之后，来我这里短暂停留一下。

paragraphs.en: I take a long pull on my cigarette. JT and I make an effort not to turn to each other. The breeze flaps against our shirts, and I press against the railing.
paragraphs.zh: 我深深吸了一口烟。JT和我都尽量不去看对方。微风拍打着我们的衬衫，我把身体靠在护栏上。

paragraphs.en: Where else? JT says. A quick trip. Or longer. I could get you a ticket.
paragraphs.zh: “还要去哪里？”JT问道。“就是短途一趟，或者更久一点。我可以给你弄张机票。”

paragraphs.en: In the bottom of the plane with the bags, I say.
paragraphs.zh: “在飞机底舱，放着行李的地方，”我说。

paragraphs.en: I’m serious, JT says. It’s a proposal. You’re American, right?
paragraphs.zh: “我不是在开玩笑，”JT说，“这是个提议。你是美国人，对吧？”

paragraphs.en: No visa needed. You can stay for three months. Easy-peasy. And your passport’s up to date?
paragraphs.zh: 不用签证。你可以停留三个月。轻而易举。你的护照是最新的吗？

paragraphs.en: Sure. Keep it in my jacket at work. In case I ever actually flip out and decide to go somewhere.
paragraphs.zh: 当然。放在我工作时的夹克口袋里。万一我真的情绪失控，决定去什么地方。

paragraphs.en: A beat passes between us. JT stretches, showing a bit of belly. When he does it a second time, I reach up his shirt until he hunches over, nearly sending us both toppling over the railing.
paragraphs.zh: 我们之间沉默了一拍。JT 伸了个懒腰，露出一点小肚腩。第二次他这样做时，我顺势拉住他的衬衫，结果他弓起身，几乎把我们两个都推向栏杆边。

paragraphs.en: Like an idea, JT says.
paragraphs.zh: 这主意不错，JT 说。

paragraphs.en: I know what you meant. Proposal sounds better.
paragraphs.zh: 我懂你什么意思。提议听起来更好。

paragraphs.en: JT grins, reaching for my dick and squeezing it. I move in to kiss him, but he blows smoke in my face and I gag.
paragraphs.zh: JT笑着，把手伸向我的鸡巴并捏了捏。我凑过去想和他接吻，但他对着我脸上吐了口烟，我呛得作呕。

paragraphs.en: That night, my brother calls. JT’s snoring, his ankles locked around mine.
paragraphs.zh: 那天晚上，我的哥哥打来电话。JT在打鼾，他的脚踝紧紧扣住了我的脚踝。

paragraphs.en: Hey, he says. I need you to pick up some food.
paragraphs.zh: 他喊道：‘喂，我需要你去买点吃的。’

paragraphs.en: I look at the time. It’s half past three.
paragraphs.zh: 我看了一眼时间。现在是三点半。

paragraphs.en: Can’t one of your friends do it? I say.
paragraphs.zh: 你一个朋友不能帮你做吗？我说。

paragraphs.en: And you don’t think I am?
paragraphs.zh: 难道你不认为我能做到？

paragraphs.en: Can you do it or not? he says.
paragraphs.zh: 你能不能做到，他说。

paragraphs.en: I can hear some kind of television through the phone. Some kind of sportscasting. JT’s snoring fills the gaps. He murmurs, just a bit, and I wonder if I’ve woken him. But he only farts.
paragraphs.zh: 我隔着手机就能听见某种电视声，像是体育节目解说。JT 的鼾声把其中的空隙填满。他轻轻地嘟囔了一下，我在想是不是把他吵醒了。可他只是放了个屁。

paragraphs.en: But after a moment he adds, You’re O.K.?
paragraphs.zh: 但过了一会儿他又补了一句：你没事吧？

paragraphs.en: You’re sure? Feeling normal?
paragraphs.zh: 你确定吗？感觉正常吗？

paragraphs.en: Really? Because you usually text. When you don’t, I get worr—
paragraphs.zh: 真的？因为你平时都会发短信。你不发的时候，我会担—

paragraphs.en: I’m good. Thanks for your concern.
paragraphs.zh: 我很好，谢关心。

paragraphs.en: Right, my brother says.
paragraphs.zh: 对，我弟弟是这么说的。

paragraphs.en: When I hang up, I lie there for another moment. I take my time slipping out from JT. He opens his eyes, briefly. But he’s still sleeping. And he closes them again, curling back into himself.
paragraphs.zh: 我挂断电话后，又躺了片刻。然后我慢慢地从JT那边离开。他短暂地睁开了眼睛，但还在睡着。接着他又把眼睛合上，缩回到自己原来的样子。

paragraphs.en: Once, years back, I was staying with my brother in our parents’ house when he got a call.
paragraphs.zh: 多年前有一次，我和弟弟住在父母家时，他接到了一个电话。

paragraphs.en: It was winter break. He was home from cooking school, flying in from New York for Christmas, and our parents were out. That evening, our family planned on ordering pizza and rewatching the same movies we’d spent every other Christmas with.
paragraphs.zh: 那是寒假。他刚从纽约回来，放学后为圣诞节从烹饪学校飞回家，而这时父母不在家。那天晚上，我们一家本来打算订比萨饼，并重看每个圣诞节都一起看的那些电影。

paragraphs.en: My brother answered the phone, then started nodding. His breathing didn’t change. But I felt his body shift, just slightly. When he hung up, he turned off the television. He told me that there’d been an accident but not to panic, because he’d take care of me.
paragraphs.zh: 我弟弟接了电话，然后开始点头。他的呼吸没有变化。但我感觉到他的身体轻轻一动。挂断电话后，他把电视关掉了。他告诉我发生了一起事故，但不要惊慌，他会照顾我的。

paragraphs.en: Neither of us moved. My brother hugged me. I didn’t know it would be for the last time. It wouldn’t happen again at the police station, or when the cops explained about the car accident, or when they asked us to identify the bodies, or at the funeral, or even when my brother announced that he’d be dropping out of the Culinary Institute of America.
paragraphs.zh: 我们谁都没有动。我的弟弟抱了抱我。我不知道这是最后一次这样的拥抱。此后无论是在警局、警察解释车祸经过时，还是在让我们辨认遗体时，在葬礼上，甚至当我弟弟宣布他要从美国烹饪学院退学时，都不会再发生。

paragraphs.en: But it was rare. I knew that then, at least. So I squeezed him back.
paragraphs.zh: 但那很少见。至少当时我知道这一点。所以我也回抱了他一下。

paragraphs.en: I’m wheeling Carl to his Paris flight when he asks me to stop. So I do.
paragraphs.zh: 我正在推着卡尔去他飞往巴黎的航班时，他让我停下。于是我就停了。

paragraphs.en: Folding his hands in his lap, he asks what love means to me.
paragraphs.zh: 他双手交叠放在腿上，问我：对你来说，爱是什么意思？

paragraphs.en: The airport isn’t especially busy. A bunch of military people shuffle through the terminal. When one of them tips his hat to Carl, the old man only snorts.
paragraphs.zh: 机场并不算特别拥挤。一群军人正在机场候机厅里慢慢走过。当其中一人向卡尔点头致意时，这位老人只是哼了一声。

paragraphs.en: Call it anthropological curiosity, Carl says.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔说，这可以叫作人类学式的好奇。

paragraphs.en: Do you have a lover? I ask.
paragraphs.zh: 你有对象吗？我问。

paragraphs.en: Too many to count, Carl says.
paragraphs.zh: 太多，卡尔说。

paragraphs.en: Are you fucking stupid? Do I not seem lovable to you?
paragraphs.zh: 你他妈是脑子有病吗？你难道不觉得我值得被爱吗？

paragraphs.en: Why the fuck else do you think I fly back here every other week?
paragraphs.zh: 你他妈以为我为什么会隔周都飞回来？

paragraphs.en: The military crowd dissolves. A gaggle of Dutch flight attendants follows them, rolling suitcases and shepherding children who skip alongside them. Carl takes a sip of his coffee, then shakes his head.
paragraphs.zh: 军人队伍散开了。一群荷兰空乘人员紧随其后，推着行李箱，带着几个边走边跳的孩子。卡尔先喝了一口咖啡，然后摇了摇头。

paragraphs.en: The best one died of AIDS, Carl says. You wouldn’t know anything about that.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔说，最强的一位死于艾滋病。你不会知道这些。

paragraphs.en: I know plenty about—
paragraphs.zh: 我对这事了如指掌——

paragraphs.en: Of course you do, Carl says. Black fag. Have to.
paragraphs.zh: 当然你知道，卡尔说。黑男同。不得不如此。

paragraphs.en: The two of us are silent for a bit.
paragraphs.zh: 我们俩沉默了一会儿。

paragraphs.en: It’s silly, Carl says. People like to say that when loved ones leave you, they’re never entirely gone. Their memory lives inside you. And you do think about them, if you’re lucky. Remember their faces. What made them laugh. How you spent your time together. But, really, that’s a selfish thing. Because they are gone. You’re the only one who benefits.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔说，这很荒唐。人们喜欢说亲人离开你后，他们并不会彻底消失，记忆仍然活在你心里。只要你走运，还会想起他们，想起他们的脸，想起是什么让他们笑，想起你们共度的时光。不过说实话，那其实是一种自私。因为他们已经不在了，受益的只有你。

paragraphs.en: Carl doesn’t say anything after that. But the monitor above us shows that it’s time for him to board. So he allows me to push him to the gate.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔说完后什么也不说了。可头顶的电子牌显示该他登机了，所以他让我把他推到登机口。

paragraphs.en: A little later, I’m with José, wheeling an old dude to his Venezuela flight. The man is talking about his new boyfriend in Caracas. His Spanish is brisk. I can pick up only bits and pieces.
paragraphs.zh: 不久之后，我和何塞一起，正在把一位老人推向他前往加拉加斯的航班。那人正在谈论他在加拉加斯的新男友。他的西班牙语说得很快，我只能听懂零碎的片段。

paragraphs.en: But what about— the old man asks, holding his hands a sizable distance apart.
paragraphs.zh: 不过会怎样——老人开口说着，双手相隔一段距离地把它们托起。

paragraphs.en: Ah, José says. Perfecto. A meal and a treat.
paragraphs.zh: “啊，”何塞说，“很好。来一顿饭和一份小食。”

paragraphs.en: It’s important, the old dude says. Remember that. The most important.
paragraphs.zh: “这是很重要的，”老人说。“记住这一点。最重要的。”

paragraphs.en: He claps and cackles until we reach the gate, then squeezes my thigh before he’s passed off to the boarding staff.
paragraphs.zh: 他拍手笑着，咯咯笑着，直到我们到达大门口，然后在被送到登机工作人员那里之前，挤了我一把大腿。

paragraphs.en: Afterward, José says, He reminds me of my dad.
paragraphs.zh: 过后，何塞说：他让我想起了我的父亲。

paragraphs.en: I thought you were Mexican, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说：我还以为你是墨西哥人。

paragraphs.en: I mean aesthetically, José says. Pendejo.
paragraphs.zh: 我说的是外表气质，何塞说。Pendejo。

paragraphs.en: Está bien. How are things with you and JT?
paragraphs.zh: Está bien。你和JT最近怎么样？

paragraphs.en: You use his full name? Jint—
paragraphs.zh: 你要叫他全名吗？Jint——

paragraphs.en: No, I say. JT’s fine. But how do you know about that?
paragraphs.zh: 我说：不，叫 JT 就行。你怎么知道这件事的？

paragraphs.en: Are you D.L.? Was it meant to be discreet?
paragraphs.zh: 你是 D.L. 吗？这本来是想保密的吗？

paragraphs.en: I blush. José looks at me as if I could not be dumber. Then he chuckles, shaking his head.
paragraphs.zh: 我脸红了。José 看着我，像是在看我到底有多笨。然后他笑了笑，摇了摇头。

paragraphs.en: Anyway, he says. You go together.
paragraphs.zh: 反正他是这么说的。你们一起走吧。

paragraphs.en: I don’t think it’s that simple.
paragraphs.zh: 我不认为事情就这么简单。

paragraphs.en: You might move a little differently, José says, but you really are just a fucking man.
paragraphs.zh: José 说，你的行动也许有点不一样，但你实际上就是个他妈的男人。

paragraphs.en: All fags are the same, José says, grinning.
paragraphs.zh: José 说着咧嘴一笑：所有基佬都一样。

paragraphs.en: I’m walking a famous white woman through the domestic terminal, alongside one of her assistants, to whom she dictates non-stop instructions about some kind of working arrangement.
paragraphs.zh: 我正和一个著名的白人女人一起走过国内航站楼，她旁边站着一个助理，助理一边听她一刻也不间断地口述着某种工作安排。

paragraphs.en: The famous woman holds a dog, some kind of mutt terrier. He hasn’t stopped barking since they entered the airport. Eventually, the famous woman announces that she needs to pee. Before the assistant wheels her away, she hands me the dog.
paragraphs.zh: 这位著名女士牵着一只杂种梗犬。自从进了机场，这只狗就一直在吠。最后，那位著名女士宣布自己需要上厕所。助理要把她推走之前，她把狗交给了我。

paragraphs.en: The mutt scoffs at me. I blink back. Half of one of his ears is torn off. Every few seconds, he adjusts himself in my arms, but finally he resigns himself to discomfort, sinking his chin onto my shoulder.
paragraphs.zh: 那只杂种犬轻蔑地瞥了我一眼。我也眨了眨眼。他的一只耳朵有一半撕裂了。每隔几秒，他就会在我怀里挪动一下身子，但最终还是忍受着不适，慢慢把下巴靠到我的肩膀上。

paragraphs.en: When the famous woman and her assistant come out, they both squint at me.
paragraphs.zh: 当那位著名女士和她的助理出来时，他们都对我眯起眼睛。

paragraphs.en: You must be a wonderful father, the famous woman says.
paragraphs.zh: 著名女性说：你一定是个了不起的父亲。

paragraphs.en: Oh, I say, I’m actually not—
paragraphs.zh: 哦，我说，我其实并不是——

paragraphs.en: He’s my baby, the famous woman says. Seen a lot. Picked him up off the street in Manhattan. Hell, he picked me up off the street. Haven’t seen him this quiet in years.
paragraphs.zh: 他是我的孩子，著名女性说。见过不少。是在曼哈顿大街上捡到他的。哎，他也是从大街上把我捡回来的。多年没见过他这么安静了。

paragraphs.en: Me either, the assistant says.
paragraphs.zh: 助理补充道：我也不是。

paragraphs.en: They both stare at me. I swallow and nod.
paragraphs.zh: 他们两人都盯着我看。我咽了口口水，点了点头。

paragraphs.en: Says a lot about you, the famous woman tells me. Now give him back.
paragraphs.zh: “这很能说明你这个人，那个著名的女人对我说。现在把他还回来。”

paragraphs.en: The famous woman extends her hands. I pass her the dog. He gives me a long look before sighing, and proceeding to bark again.
paragraphs.zh: 著名的女人伸出手来。我把狗递给她。它先看了我很久，叹了口气，然后又开始吠叫。

paragraphs.en: I invite JT to dinner at my place, wholly expecting him to decline. But he accepts. So I find myself at home an hour earlier than usual, telling my brother that I’m hosting a meal.
paragraphs.zh: 我邀请JT来我家吃晚饭，完全预期他会拒绝。但他接受了。于是我比平时早一小时到家，告诉我的兄弟我在家准备了一顿饭。

paragraphs.en: He’s still in last night’s clothes, a Salah jersey and joggers. Doesn’t look up from his phone. He groans.
paragraphs.zh: 他还穿着昨晚的衣服，一件萨拉赫球衣和一条运动裤。没有从手机上抬头看我。他发出一声闷哼。

paragraphs.en: Yes, I say. With a man.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，‘对。’ 一个男人。

paragraphs.en: My brother scrunches his face up. Then he relaxes.
paragraphs.zh: 我弟弟皱起了脸。然后他放松下来。

paragraphs.en: Heard, he says. I’ll see myself out.
paragraphs.zh: ‘听见了。’他开口道。‘我自己先走。’

paragraphs.en: Wait, I say. Really?
paragraphs.zh: 等等，我说。真的吗？

paragraphs.en: Do you think I’m some sort of sentry?
paragraphs.zh: 你是不是把我当成某种守卫了？

paragraphs.en: One of the boys will pick me up, he says. Text when you’re done.
paragraphs.zh: 他会派一个男生来接我，等你做完了给我发短信。

paragraphs.en: But what will you do?
paragraphs.zh: 那你要怎么办？

paragraphs.en: My brother stares at me, narrowing his eyes.
paragraphs.zh: 我弟弟盯着我看，眯起眼睛。

paragraphs.en: That’s your problem, he says. Worried about everyone but yourself.
paragraphs.zh: 他说：这正是你的问题。你担心的是别人，不是自己。

paragraphs.en: I start to tell him that he’s wrong. I am worried about absolutely everything, including myself, two hundred per cent of the time. Everything and everyone horrifies me. Nothing in this life is stable. Glass houses built on quicksand. It’s why we live together, and why I haven’t left home, and why I haven’t ever taken a flight of my own, despite the fact that I babysit people who fly all over the fucking world every day.
paragraphs.zh: 我想告诉他他错了。我的担心几乎覆盖了我对所有事情的全部注意力，包括我自己，那是百分之两百的程度。所有的一切和每一个人都让我感到恐惧。生活里没有什么是稳定的。就像建在流沙上的玻璃屋。这就是我们住在一起的原因，也是我没有离开家的原因；也是我虽然每天照看着那些每天飞遍世界各地的人，却始终没有坐过一架我自己的飞机的原因。

paragraphs.en: But I do not say any of that.
paragraphs.zh: 但我什么都没说。

paragraphs.en: Cool, my brother says. Have fun.
paragraphs.zh: 行啊，我哥说。玩得开心。

paragraphs.en: Thirty minutes later, JT appears at the door.
paragraphs.zh: 三十分钟后，JT出现在门口。

paragraphs.en: He’s still in his work clothes. Holding a backpack in one hand and a bag of Peruvian takeout in the other.
paragraphs.zh: 他还穿着工作服，左手拿着一个背包，右手拿着一袋秘鲁外卖。

paragraphs.en: A gift, he says, offering me the food.
paragraphs.zh: 他边说“这是个礼物”，边把食物递给我。

paragraphs.en: Ah, I say. A whole chicken?
paragraphs.zh: 啊，我说。整只鸡？

paragraphs.en: I thought we were cooking.
paragraphs.zh: 我还以为我们在做饭呢。

paragraphs.en: We aren’t, JT says, closing the door behind him, and it’s only a few moments later that we’re fucking.
paragraphs.zh: 我们并没有，JT说着，关上了身后的门，过了没多久我们就开始做爱了。

paragraphs.en: Afterward, naked under a blanket on the living-room sofa, JT asks if I’ve thought about Taipei.
paragraphs.zh: 事后，俩人赤裸着躺在客厅的沙发上盖着毯子，JT问我有没有想过台北。

paragraphs.en: Oh, I say. You were serious.
paragraphs.zh: “啊，真是的，你是认真的吗？”

paragraphs.en: I’m not a clown, JT says. It would be nice, you know. Come for a month.
paragraphs.zh: “我不是小丑，JT说。你知道吗，这会很好。至少来一个月。”

paragraphs.en: Yes. This is my formal proposition. Do you have vacation time left?
paragraphs.zh: “是的，这是我正式的提议。你还有剩余假期吗？”

paragraphs.en: Sure. Not like I have a reason to use it.
paragraphs.zh: “当然了。又不是我没事可做还要去用假期。”

paragraphs.en: So it’s no problem. Spend a week at my place. I’ll take the days off, too. We’ll travel down the coast and back again.
paragraphs.zh: 那倒没问题。来我这儿住一周吧。我也会请假。我们沿着海岸线去一趟，然后再回来。

paragraphs.en: JT doesn’t look at me when he says this. He’s fidgeting with his fingers, which is new. It’s hard to tell, exactly, how serious he is. I rub his knuckles until he reaches for my palm and presses gently.
paragraphs.zh: JT说这话时没看我。他在摆弄着手指，这还是第一次。很难准确判断他到底有多认真。我搓着他的指关节，直到他去拿我的手掌并轻轻按下去。

paragraphs.en: It’s a long plane ride, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，这是个很长的航程。

paragraphs.en: Bug your P.C.P. for a Valium, JT says.
paragraphs.zh: JT说，去让你那位P.C.P.给你拿些Valium。

paragraphs.en: You think I have a doctor?
paragraphs.zh: 你以为我有医生吗？

paragraphs.en: So you two are friends.
paragraphs.zh: 那你们两个是朋友。

paragraphs.en: JT turns to me. He yawns, slowly. Then he presses his head against my chest, squeezing my thigh with his free hand. I do the same.
paragraphs.zh: JT转向我。 他慢慢打了个哈欠。 然后他把头靠在我的胸口，用另一只手挤了下我的大腿。 我也照着做了。

paragraphs.en: I’m leaving for a few months, he says.
paragraphs.zh: 他一边说着，一边说他要离开几个月。

paragraphs.en: JT chuckles and pinches me. I shift my weight, until we’re pressed closer together.
paragraphs.zh: JT轻笑着捏了我一下。我调整了身子，直到我们贴得更近。

paragraphs.en: Doing some work with the airline back home, JT says. At least until the summer. Probably.
paragraphs.zh: JT说他回国后要在那家航空公司做一些工作。至少会持续到夏天。大概吧。

paragraphs.en: Yes. So, you know, we won’t be seeing each other for a while.
paragraphs.zh: 是啊，所以你也知道，我们一段时间都见不到面了。

paragraphs.en: But you’ll come back? I ask.
paragraphs.zh: “那你会回来吗？”我问。

paragraphs.en: Mm, JT says. You never know with these things. I’m starting to miss Taiwan, a little bit. Edible food. Public transit. Boba that doesn’t cost a brick. But, if I want, I can take the next few weeks off, you know, to adjust. I will have some disposable time.
paragraphs.zh: 嗯，JT说。碰到这些事你永远不知道怎么回事。我开始有点想念台湾了。可以吃的食物。公共交通。不会贵得离谱的珍珠奶茶。不过，如果我想的话，接下来几周我可以请假来调整一下。我会有一些可以支配的时间。

paragraphs.en: JT turns to look at me. I peer back. I know that now would be the moment to say something reassuring, or comforting, but I don’t know what the words will taste like when they leave my mouth.
paragraphs.zh: JT转过头看着我。我也回望着他。我知道这时正是说些安慰、或令他安心的话的瞬间，但我不知道这些词从我嘴里说出来后会是什么味道。

paragraphs.en: It isn’t much longer before JT’s snoring.
paragraphs.zh: 不久，JT就开始打起了呼噜。

paragraphs.en: And then I’m out, too.
paragraphs.zh: 然后，我也睡过去了。

paragraphs.en: A few hours later, I wake up to find my brother leering from the kitchen counter.
paragraphs.zh: 几小时后，我醒来时发现我弟弟正站在厨房台面旁边盯着我看。

paragraphs.en: He’s watching us, silently. Tapping at his phone. I don’t know how long he’s been there or what he’s looking for. But he snorts, once, before he stands and saunters down the hall.
paragraphs.zh: 他沉默地看着我们，不时摸着手机。我不知道他在那儿待了多久，也不知道他在找什么。但他先轻哼了一声，然后站起身，慢悠悠地沿着走廊走了下去。

paragraphs.en: The next morning, I’m walking Carl through the airport, and he’s in as light of a mood as ever. Commenting on people’s coats. Snickering at their shoes. A T.S.A. agent asks him to stand from his wheelchair. Carl says that he’ll do it for fifty dollars.
paragraphs.zh: 第二天早上，我带着卡尔走过机场，他的情绪和往常一样轻松。还在评论人们的外套，笑着嘲弄他们的鞋子。一个T.S.A.特工叫他从轮椅上站起来。卡尔说他要五十美元才肯站。

paragraphs.en: JT left my place before I woke up. Didn’t say goodbye. But he sent me a text with his address, some dates, and several milk-tea emojis.
paragraphs.zh: JT在我醒来前离开了我这里。没有说再见。但他给我发了一条短信，里面有他的地址、一些日期，以及几个奶茶表情符号。

paragraphs.en: And also: a flight voucher.
paragraphs.zh: 还有：一张机票代金券。

paragraphs.en: And the dates he saved for the ticket.
paragraphs.zh: 以及他为机票预留的日期。

paragraphs.en: There’s a long layover in Tokyo.
paragraphs.zh: 东京会有一段很长的中转停留。

paragraphs.en: All you have to do is book it, he wrote. Or don’t.
paragraphs.zh: 他写道：你要做的就只是在上面订票。要么就别订。

paragraphs.en: Also, he wrote, your brother is cute.
paragraphs.zh: 他还写道，你的弟弟很可爱。

paragraphs.en: Carl and I are taking our usual path through the terminal when we pass the public piano. Carl sets a palm on my hand, bringing us to a stop.
paragraphs.zh: 我和卡尔走在我们平常穿过大厅时会经过的那架公共钢琴旁。卡尔把手掌放到我手上，示意我们停下。

paragraphs.en: Then Carl stands and hobbles toward the instrument. I watch as he stretches his fingers. Runs them across the keys. And then, gently, he begins to play.
paragraphs.zh: 接着卡尔站起来，蹒跚着走向那件乐器。我看见他舒展手指、在琴键上来回滑动，然后轻轻地开始弹起了曲子。

paragraphs.en: A few passersby pause to listen. A lady holds her toddler’s hand, wagging it along with the music. But Carl doesn’t pay any of us any mind.
paragraphs.zh: 有几位路过的人停下脚步听着。一位女士牵着她的幼儿，在音乐里轻轻摆动着手。可卡尔却对我们谁也不理会。

paragraphs.en: Some birds glide above us, en route from one terminal to the next. They linger for a minute, circling Carl, and then they’re gone.
paragraphs.zh: 有些鸟儿在我们上方掠过，从一个航站楼飞向下一个航站楼。它们在卡尔上空停留了一会儿，盘旋片刻后又消失了。

paragraphs.en: Eventually, the music stops.
paragraphs.zh: 最终，音乐停了。

paragraphs.en: Carl stands, slowly. The woman and her kid start to clap. I do, too. And Carl gives us a long look before taking a bow.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔慢慢站起身来。那位女士和她的孩子开始鼓掌。我也跟着拍手。卡尔看着我们看了一会儿，然后鞠了一躬。

paragraphs.en: A few days later, a Black woman I’m wheeling to an Amsterdam flight is dozing in her chair when I ask if I can tell her a story.
paragraphs.zh: 几天后，我推着一位黑人女士去阿姆斯特丹的航班，她在椅子上打瞌睡，这时我问她可否给她讲个故事。

paragraphs.en: Her braids are long, tucked under a knit cap. She looks up at me, squinting.
paragraphs.zh: 她的辫子很长，塞在一顶针织帽下面。她抬起头看着我，眯起眼。

paragraphs.en: You didn’t see me sleeping? she asks.
paragraphs.zh: 你没看到我在睡觉吗？她问。

paragraphs.en: You’re fine, she says, waving her hand. What’ve you got?
paragraphs.zh: 你没事吧？她一边说一边挥手。你有事吗？

paragraphs.en: Once, I say, there was a boy who I worked with.
paragraphs.zh: 从前有个男孩，我曾和他共事。

paragraphs.en: We worked together in the evening. Sometimes the airport was busy. But sometimes it wasn’t. On the empty days, we’d lie down on the floor and stare up at the sky through the window above.
paragraphs.zh: 我们晚上一起值班。有时机场很忙，但有时并不忙。那些冷清的日子，我们会躺在地板上，透过上方的窗户望着天空。

paragraphs.en: But, I say, one day we found a bird in the terminal.
paragraphs.zh: 但有一天，我们在航站楼里发现了一只鸟。

paragraphs.en: A bird, the woman says.
paragraphs.zh: 那位女士说，是一只鸟。

paragraphs.en: She nods for me to continue.
paragraphs.zh: 她朝我点点头，示意我继续。

paragraphs.en: His wing was clipped, I say. I didn’t want to hold him, because of the possibility, you know, of disease. But my friend didn’t care about that. That didn’t matter to him. So he picked up the bird, and he asked me to call a car, and I did. We rode together to the vet clinic. It was, like, four in the morning. Behind the desk, a sleepy attendant eyed us. But then we were called into a room where the bird was checked out. We were told that the clinic would take care of him, but my friend asked if we could receive updates, so for the next few weeks this vet sent us videos of the bird. Moving tentatively. Flapping around. And then, like a month later, the bird was released.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，他的翅膀是折断了。我不想抱他，怕会有传染疾病的可能。可我朋友根本不在意，这点对他毫无意义。所以他把那只鸟捡起来，让我去叫辆车，我照做了。我们一起坐车去宠物诊所。那时大概是凌晨四点。柜台后面有位困倦的接待员打量着我们。然后我们被带进一间房，鸟在那儿被检查。我们被告知诊所会照顾它，但我朋友问我们能否收到更新，于是接下来几个星期这位兽医都会发来它的视频。它在那儿动作很谨慎，拍着翅膀。大概一个月后，鸟被放飞了。

paragraphs.en: We even named him, I say. But the thing is, I don’t remember what we decided on.
paragraphs.zh: 我们甚至给它起了名字，我说。可问题是，我都不记得我们最后决定叫什么了。

paragraphs.en: A crowd of young tennis players passes us, jostling and laughing. Their coaches try to corral them. When I look down at the woman, I see that she’s fallen back asleep.
paragraphs.zh: 一群年轻的网球运动员从我们身边经过，挤来挤去，笑着闹着。教练们试图把他们拢在一起来。等我低头看那位女士时，看到她已经又睡着了。

paragraphs.en: Which is for the best, probably.
paragraphs.zh: 这大概是最好的结果。

paragraphs.en: So I continue wheeling her to the gate. But then she reaches up, slowly, and rests a hand on mine, rubbing it with her thumb.
paragraphs.zh: 所以我继续把她推着往门口走。但她慢慢伸出手，放到我的手上，用拇指摩挲着。

paragraphs.en: Once, a few years ago, I was walking back from a gate where I had dropped off a passenger when I saw a guy lying on the floor.
paragraphs.zh: 几年前有一次，我从一处下客点把一位乘客送走后正往回走，结果看到一个人躺在地上。

paragraphs.en: His arms and legs were splayed out. You see a lot of shit after midnight, but this guy had on a flight-attendant uniform. I thought about checking on him, but that didn’t seem worth it. Probably had his own shit going on.
paragraphs.zh: 他的手脚都摊开着。半夜以后你会看到不少烂摊子，但这家伙穿着空乘制服。我当时想去看看他一下，不过似乎不值得。可能是他自己有事。

paragraphs.en: Then I changed my mind. Turned around and walked back.
paragraphs.zh: 后来我又改变了主意，转身走了回去。

paragraphs.en: Standing over JT, I asked what was wrong.
paragraphs.zh: 我站在JT面前，问他出了什么事。

paragraphs.en: I fucked up today, he said.
paragraphs.zh: 他说：我今天搞砸了。

paragraphs.en: Cussed out a passenger.
paragraphs.zh: 我对一名乘客破口大骂了。

paragraphs.en: Not really. No. But I think you’ll be fine.
paragraphs.zh: 其实没什么。不，没事。不过我想你会没事的。

paragraphs.en: Maybe, JT said. I also might lose my job.
paragraphs.zh: “也许吧，”JT 说。“我也可能会失去这份工作。”

paragraphs.en: O.K., I said. You’ll get another one.
paragraphs.zh: “好吧，”我说。“你会再找到一份。”

paragraphs.en: You’re pretty confident, he said.
paragraphs.zh: “你非常有把握，”他说。

paragraphs.en: Trust me, I said. I’m not.
paragraphs.zh: “相信我，”我说。“我没有。”

paragraphs.en: I sat beside JT. Don’t know why.
paragraphs.zh: 我坐在JT旁边。也不知道为什么。

paragraphs.en: So you need to lie on the ground? I asked.
paragraphs.zh: “所以你要躺在地上吗？”我问。

paragraphs.en: It helps me. Puts things in perspective. How often do you actually look up?
paragraphs.zh: 它对我有帮助。能让事情看起来更有尺度。你实际上有多久抬起头看一眼？

paragraphs.en: There isn’t much to see.
paragraphs.zh: 没什么可看的。

paragraphs.en: JT turned to me. He seemed genuinely curious. Then he patted the space beside him.
paragraphs.zh: JT 转向我，神情显得是真心好奇。然后他拍了拍旁边的位置。

paragraphs.en: I frowned. But then, nearly involuntarily, I lay down, too. It really was the first time that I’d looked up at the airport.
paragraphs.zh: 我皱了皱眉头。但随后，我几乎是下意识地也躺了下去。那真是我第一次抬头看机场。

paragraphs.en: But also, probably, the first time anywhere, in a long, long time.
paragraphs.zh: 而且，也许是很久很久以来，我在任何地方的第一次。

paragraphs.en: Nothing to report, I said. But it did look a bit different.
paragraphs.zh: 我说没有什么可汇报的。不过看起来确实有点不一样。

paragraphs.en: O.K., JT said. But let’s stay a bit longer. Just to make sure.
paragraphs.zh: “好，JT 说。‘不过我们再待一会儿，确认一下。’

paragraphs.en: The morning after JT returns to Taiwan, I’m lying on the sofa, dozing, when my brother opens the front door and steps out of his shoes.
paragraphs.zh: 在 JT 回到台湾的第二天早上，我正躺在沙发上打盹儿，这时我哥打开了前门，刚脱下鞋子走出来。

paragraphs.en: He takes one look at me. Then he looks again.
paragraphs.zh: 他看了我一眼，又看了一眼。

paragraphs.en: You’re off today? he asks.
paragraphs.zh: “你今天不上班吗？”他问。

paragraphs.en: And you’re just spending it here?
paragraphs.zh: 你就要把钱都花在这里吗？

paragraphs.en: Where else would I go?
paragraphs.zh: 我还能去哪里？

paragraphs.en: My brother smirks at me. Stuffs his hands in the pocket of his hoodie.
paragraphs.zh: 我哥哥对我轻蔑地笑了笑，把双手塞进连帽衫的口袋里。

paragraphs.en: What about that guy? he asks. Your boyf—
paragraphs.zh: 那那个家伙呢？他问。你的男友——

paragraphs.en: I don’t have one of those, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，我没有那种东西。

paragraphs.en: My brother sighs. Then he walks into the kitchen, where he opens drawers and pulls a tub of cold rice from the fridge. He lines the counter with vegetables, condiments, and a plastic container of minced crab.
paragraphs.zh: 我弟弟叹了口气。然后他走进厨房，从冰箱里打开抽屉拿出一盒冷饭。他把案台摆上了蔬菜、调味品和一只装碎蟹的塑料盒。

paragraphs.en: Eventually, my brother comes back into the living room and drops a bowl in front of me. It’s fried rice, topped with chile oil and scallions and fried shallots, stuffed with crab and carrots and eggs and onions and peas and herbs. He settles across from me, cross-legged, sipping from a beer can and picking at a bowl of nuts.
paragraphs.zh: 过了一会儿，我弟弟走回客厅，在我面前放下一个碗。那是炒饭，上面撒着辣椒油、葱花和炸香葱，里面有蟹肉、胡萝卜、鸡蛋、洋葱和豌豆，还有香草。他对坐在我对面，盘腿而坐，啜着一罐啤酒，边吃一碗坚果。

paragraphs.en: We sit in front of the television. Leopards chase a gazelle across the screen.
paragraphs.zh: 我们坐在电视前。萤幕上，豹子正追着瞪羚狂奔。

paragraphs.en: It’s fine that you live here, my brother says.
paragraphs.zh: “你在这儿住得很好，”我的哥哥说。

paragraphs.en: I’m serious, my brother says. Routine is good. I don’t mind checking on you. Giving you shit to do. Even if it is more for you than for me.
paragraphs.zh: 我是在认真的。我的哥哥说：有规律的生活是件好事。我不介意来看你，也不介意给你安排点活干。就算那更多是为了你，而不是为了我。

paragraphs.en: I don’t say anything to this. My brother burps, pulling from his beer.
paragraphs.zh: 我对此不作声。我的哥哥喝着啤酒，打了个嗝。

paragraphs.en: But, he says, I think, you know, it isn’t every day that you have the chance to leave. And not just to leave but to have someone who wants you to follow them. That’s rare, you know. Doesn’t happen often.
paragraphs.zh: 他又说：我想，你也明白，并不是每天都有机会离开。更不是说离开，而是能有人愿意让你跟着走。那种事很少见，你知道的，毕竟不常发生。

paragraphs.en: How the fuck do you know that?
paragraphs.zh: 你他妈怎么知道的？

paragraphs.en: Your boyfriend mentioned it the other day. Before he took off.
paragraphs.zh: 你男朋友前几天提过这事。就在他离开前。

paragraphs.en: Fuck, nigga. Got it. Maybe you should just stay here.
paragraphs.zh: 靠，兄弟。明白了。也许你就应该待在这儿。

paragraphs.en: My shoulders tense up. I feel like I should stand. And also like I’m stuck. I take another mouthful of fried rice, allowing its flavors to settle across my tongue.
paragraphs.zh: 我的肩膀绷紧了。我觉得自己应该站起来。也觉得自己像是被卡住了。我又吃了一口炒饭，让味道在舌头上慢慢散开。

paragraphs.en: I don’t know what’ll happen if I leave, I say.
paragraphs.zh: 我说我不知道我离开以后会怎样。

paragraphs.en: My brother bites his lip. He turns to me, snorting.
paragraphs.zh: 我弟弟咬着嘴唇。他转向我，哼了一声。

paragraphs.en: I said, So fucking what? You think anyone does? Don’t give everyone else so much credit. You have to move to find out where the motion will take you. It’s one thing to try without knowing, maybe. It’s another thing not to try at all. That’s for dumbasses. Or rich people.
paragraphs.zh: 我说，『那又怎样，妈的？你以为有谁会呢？别给别人想得太聪明。你得去行动，才会知道这种举动会带你到哪里。尝试一下在不知道结果时也许还能算，问题是完全不去尝试。这才是蠢货，或有钱人干的事。』

paragraphs.en: My brother shakes his head. Then he reaches for my bowl, stacking his beer and his own bowl inside it, dropping them onto the kitchen counter, and stepping back into his shoes and leaving me again.
paragraphs.zh: 我弟弟摇了摇头。接着他去拿我的碗，把自己的啤酒和碗一起放进去，掉在厨房的橱柜台上，然后又穿回鞋子，再次离开了我。

paragraphs.en: A few days later, I’m walking Carl through the airport when we run into this kid. The terminal’s crowded. Foot traffic flows around us.
paragraphs.zh: 几天后，我和卡尔在机场散步时遇到了这个孩子。候机厅很拥挤。人流在我们身边川流不息。

paragraphs.en: The boy’s standing alone. Looking fucking lost. Holding a backpack in one hand and a Nintendo Switch in the other. He can’t be much older than five or six.
paragraphs.zh: 这个男孩一个人站着，看起来非常迷茫。左手拿着一个背包，右手拿着一台任天堂Switch。他看起来不大于五六岁。

paragraphs.en: This kid looks up at the two of us, sniffling. Carl turns from him to me.
paragraphs.zh: 男孩抬头看着我们两个，边抽鼻子。卡尔从他那边转向我。

paragraphs.en: Should we say something? I ask.
paragraphs.zh: 他问：我们要不要说点什么？我说道。

paragraphs.en: Why the fuck are you asking me? Don’t you fucking work here?
paragraphs.zh: 你他妈问我干嘛？你他妈不是在这儿上班吗？

paragraphs.en: I push the wheelchair over to the kid. He looks skeptical, wiping at his face. I ask where he’s supposed to be going, and he only sniffles back at me. He says something, and then something else, and Carl says, Japanese.
paragraphs.zh: 我把轮椅推到那个孩子面前。他看起来很怀疑，一边擦着脸。我问他要去哪里，他只对我轻轻抽泣了声。然后他说了点什么，又说了点什么，卡尔说：日语。

paragraphs.en: Carl glares at me. Then his gaze softens, settling into a face I didn’t even know he could make.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔瞪了我一眼。随后他的目光缓和下来，变成了我以前都不知道他能露出的一种表情。

paragraphs.en: He speaks sweetly, slowly to the kid. Totally different voice.
paragraphs.zh: 他对着那个孩子说话，语气又甜又慢。完全是另一种声音。

paragraphs.en: The boy gawks. Then he says something, and then again.
paragraphs.zh: 这个男孩目瞪口呆。然后他说了点什么，又说了一次。

paragraphs.en: Carl says something else to him. The kid manages to giggle.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔又对他说了另一句话。这个孩子勉强笑了出来。

paragraphs.en: Boy’s looking for his mother, Carl says. Went to the bathroom and lost her.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔说，这个男孩在找他妈妈。去洗手间时把她弄丢了。

paragraphs.en: Shit, I say. Does he know what gate he’s flying out of?
paragraphs.zh: 我说："见鬼。" 他知道自己是从哪个登机口起飞的吗？

paragraphs.en: Carl looks at me like I’m a fucking idiot.
paragraphs.zh: Carl 看着我，仿佛我就是个该死的白痴。

paragraphs.en: You can’t be serious, he says.
paragraphs.zh: “你不会是认真的吗？”他这么说。

paragraphs.en: You’re about to miss your flight, I say.
paragraphs.zh: “你快要误机了。”我说。

paragraphs.en: Then I’ll miss the flight, Carl says.
paragraphs.zh: Carl 说：“那我就会误机了。”

paragraphs.en: Carl sighs. Then he crosses his arms.
paragraphs.zh: 卡尔叹了口气。然后他交叉双臂。

paragraphs.en: Shit happens, he says. Do you think you’re the only one who works at the airport? I will find my own way if that is what I have to do, because that is simply what I will have to do. We adjust. That’s life.
paragraphs.zh: “见过这种事，”他道，“你以为只有你一个人在机场上班吗？如果我不得不自己想办法，我就会想出办法，因为那只能是我必须做的事。我们会适应的。生活就是这样。”

paragraphs.en: Realize that I’ve stopped breathing.
paragraphs.zh: 我意识到自己已经不再呼吸了。

paragraphs.en: The kid looks from Carl to me and back, sniffling.
paragraphs.zh: 那个孩子从卡尔那里看向我，又看回我这边，边抽泣边吸鼻子。

paragraphs.en: So I grab Carl’s wheelchair. And Carl talks to the kid, holding his hand as we walk.
paragraphs.zh: 于是我拿起了卡尔的轮椅。卡尔一边和这个孩子说话，一边拉着他的手，我们走着。

paragraphs.en: The three of us step through the airport. From time to time, someone turns our way, but nobody stops us. We arrive at a screen displaying the departures and find one headed for Tokyo, and we walk there, halfway across the airport.
paragraphs.zh: 我们三人走过机场。时不时会有人看向我们，但没人拦我们。我们走到显示起飞信息的电子屏前，找到一班飞往东京的航班，然后往那边走去，穿过了机场的一半。

paragraphs.en: The kid chats and chats. Babbling. Carl answers occasionally, softly, full of warmth. We’ve nearly made it to the gate when we hear, all of a sudden, a shriek.
paragraphs.zh: 孩子唠唠叨叨地聊个不停。卡尔偶尔会轻声回答，语气温和而亲切。我们几乎要到了登机口时，突然听到一声尖叫。

paragraphs.en: The kid’s mother sprints over. The father jogs behind her. She tugs on the kid’s arm, gently, while he smothers his nose into her shoulder, and this woman nods deeply to Carl before he waves her off.
paragraphs.zh: 孩子的母亲冲了过来，父亲在她身后快步追着。她轻轻拽住孩子的胳膊，孩子却把鼻子埋进了她的肩膀；在卡尔向她挥手示意不要过来前，这位女士便深深地点了点头。

paragraphs.en: She turns to me. I shake my head. She makes a face I can’t quite read, grinning, before she turns around. Their little family clusters together. Then the kid turns back to us, waving, and the trio rushes toward their boarding gate.
paragraphs.zh: 她转向我。我摇了摇头。她做了个我看不太懂的表情，笑了笑后转身。小家伙们成群地挤在一起。然后那个孩子又转过来朝我们挥手，三人一同冲向登机口。

paragraphs.en: Which leaves Carl and me alone. Passengers start assembling beside the gate, tapping at their phones.
paragraphs.zh: 于是，卡尔和我就只剩我们两个人了。乘客开始在登机口旁边聚拢，低头在手机上刷着。

paragraphs.en: A thought forms for me, briefly. It disappears just as quickly.
paragraphs.zh: 我脑中一闪而过一个想法，转瞬即逝。

paragraphs.en: But, as if he’s heard it, Carl clucks his tongue.
paragraphs.zh: 但仿佛他听见了那念头，卡尔便发出咂舌声。

paragraphs.en: Why wouldn’t you? he says.
paragraphs.zh: “你为什么不愿意呢？”他问道。

paragraphs.en: You heard me, Carl says.
paragraphs.zh: “你听到我刚才说的吗，”卡尔说。

paragraphs.en: And I think about that.
paragraphs.zh: 而我在想这一点。

paragraphs.en: I will find my way, he says.
paragraphs.zh: “我会找到自己的路，”他说。

paragraphs.en: I give him a blank stare. Check my watch. And I look at Carl as I step to the flight counter. I look at him as the woman scrunches her nose, scans my voucher, flipping through my passport, shaking her head, until she says, Oh. There’s a seat. You really don’t have any bags?
paragraphs.zh: 我目光呆滞地盯着他。看了看手表。走到登机柜台时，我看向Carl。看着那位女士皱着鼻子、扫描我的机票、翻开护照，一边摇头，直到她说：哦，有个空位。你真的一个行李也没带吗？

paragraphs.en: No, I say. I really don’t.
paragraphs.zh: 我说：没有，我真的一个都没有。

paragraphs.en: I turn back to Carl.
paragraphs.zh: 我转身看向Carl。

paragraphs.en: Then, finally, as I step toward the tunnel, I wave.
paragraphs.zh: 然后，终于，当我朝隧道走去时，我朝他挥了挥手。

paragraphs.en: And Carl, finally, waves back. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 最后，卡尔也向他挥了挥手。♦
